methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
BurnBot, a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $25M to date, is valued within a frontier-tech sector represented by a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparator (LNZA). The three most directly comparable Series A peers—Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy—provide a baseline for relative valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied value of $50M. Peer-median analysis draws on the three same-stage, same-sector comparables, which trade at a median valuation of $150M with a range spanning $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte). This method implies $150M.

The two methods produce a wide dispersion, with stage-progression implying $50M and peer-median implying $150M. The triangulated range is $42.5M (low) to $172.5M (high), with a median of $100M. The substantial gap between methods reflects underlying uncertainty around whether BurnBot's capital efficiency and progress stage more closely resemble its lower-funded trajectory or the maturity of its peer set. Key uncertainties include the degree to which peer valuations reflect comparable technology risk, customer traction, and path to capital efficiency, all of which would narrow the valuation range materially with additional diligence.
